name: kbridge-slider-crank-animate
description: Phase-4 of the animate plan — the earned-trust payoff: Inventor STEP geometry (converted to part-local mm .3dm in KinematicBridge/out/slider-crank/bodies/) bound to the bridge-translated slider-crank .kin.json and animated through CPig's OWN solve (Read Mechanism -> Bind Body From File x4 -> Sweep(Live) -> Animate Bound -> Custom Preview), posed mid-sweep at Step 17/34 (~85 deg). Sweep overridden at the node to 0 -> 2.967 rad (170 deg) to avoid the 180 deg dead-center (B-cand-008). Pass/fail = Converged + SlopSuccess asserts (guards B-bug-008 end-to-end with real geometry). Runs at doc=MM like the fork pipeline smoke: Custom Preview draws Animate Bound's raw canonical-mm geometry with no unit awareness, and there is no importer in this graph to co-locate with (cm + World Frame is the Phase-5 side-by-side). CPig-only; no KinematicImporter .gha needed. Slop: KinematicBridge/out/_slop-side-by-side/slider-crank-bind-sweep-animate.canary.slop.json.

setup.vlmDescription: A slider-crank mechanism shown as shaded solid CAD parts (a base rail, a short crank arm, a connecting rod, and a flat slider plate) posed mid-motion with the crank rotated about 85 degrees, overlaid with a small CPig frame-triad skeleton. The solid parts sit on the skeleton joints, forming one connected mechanism at a sane scale.

checkpoints.name: persp
checkpoints.description: Inventor slider-crank CAD parts posed mid-sweep by CPig's solve, on the CPig skeleton.

checkpoints.name: front
checkpoints.description: Front view of the CPig-posed slider-crank parts.
